an3.59:1.1: Then the brahmin Jāṇussoṇi approached the *Bhagavā*; having approached, he … sat to one side. Seated to one side, the brahmin Jāṇussoṇi said this to the *Bhagavā*:
an3.59:1.2: "Master Gotama, whoever might give a gift — whether from a sacrifice, a memorial offering, a dish-offering, or a gift-worthy matter — should give it to brahmins of the three knowledges."
an3.59:1.3: "But how, brahmin, do brahmins designate one who has the three knowledges?"
an3.59:1.4: "Here, Master Gotama, a brahmin is well-born on both sides, from mother and father, of pure lineage for seven generations of ancestors, unchallenged and unimpeached in terms of birth; a reciter, a bearer of sacred hymns; a master of the three Vedas together with their vocabularies, ritual texts, phonology, and history as the fifth — skilled in metre, in grammar, and proficient in natural philosophy and the marks of a great man.
an3.59:1.5: In this way, Master Gotama, brahmins designate one who has the three knowledges."
an3.59:10.1: "In this way, brahmin, one has the three knowledges in the *vinaya* of the noble ones."
an3.59:10.2: "It is one thing, Master Gotama, to have the three knowledges among brahmins, and another to have the three knowledges in the *vinaya* of the noble ones.
an3.59:10.3: And, Master Gotama, the three knowledges of brahmins are not worth a sixteenth part of the three knowledges in the *vinaya* of the noble ones.
an3.59:11.2: Let Master Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge from this day forth, for life."
an3.59:2.1: "It is one thing, brahmin, for brahmins to designate a brahmin as one of the three knowledges, and another to have the three knowledges in the *vinaya* of the noble ones."
an3.59:2.2: "But how, Master Gotama, does one have the three knowledges in the *vinaya* of the noble ones?
an3.59:2.3: *Sādhu*, Master Gotama — would that you teach the *dhamma* in such a way as to show how one has the three knowledges in the *vinaya* of the noble ones."
an3.59:2.4: "Well then, brahmin, listen and pay close attention; I will speak."
an3.59:2.5: "Yes, sir," the brahmin Jāṇussoṇi replied to the *Bhagavā*.
an3.59:3.1: "Here, brahmin, a *bhikkhu*, quite secluded from sensual pleasures …
an3.59:3.2: enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*.
an3.59:4.1: With his mind [*citta*] thus endowed with *samādhi*, pure, bright, unblemished, free from corruption, malleable, wieldy, steady, and having attained imperturbability, he directs his mind [*citta*] to the knowledge of recollecting past abodes.
an3.59:4.2: He recollects many kinds of past abodes — namely: one birth, two births … — thus, with their aspects and particulars, he recollects many kinds of past abodes.
an3.59:4.3: This is his first knowledge attained;
an3.59:4.4: blindness [*avijjā*] is destroyed, knowledge has arisen; darkness is destroyed, light has arisen — as it comes to one who dwells diligent, ardent, and resolute.
an3.59:5.1: With his mind [*citta*] thus endowed with *samādhi*, pure, bright, unblemished, free from corruption, malleable, wieldy, steady, and having attained imperturbability, he directs his mind [*citta*] to the knowledge of the passing away and reappearance of beings.
an3.59:5.2: With the divine eye, purified and surpassing the human … he understands beings according to their *kamma*.
an3.59:5.3: This is his second knowledge attained;
an3.59:5.4: blindness [*avijjā*] is destroyed, knowledge has arisen; darkness is destroyed, light has arisen — as it comes to one who dwells diligent, ardent, and resolute.
an3.59:6.1: With his mind [*citta*] thus endowed with *samādhi*, pure, bright, unblemished, free from corruption, malleable, wieldy, steady, and having attained imperturbability, he directs his mind [*citta*] to the knowledge of the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an3.59:6.2: He understands as it truly is: 'This is *dukkha*' … 'This is the path leading to the cessation of *dukkha*' he understands as it truly is;
an3.59:6.3: 'These are the *āsavas*' he understands as it truly is … 'This is the path leading to the cessation of the *āsavas*' he understands as it truly is.
an3.59:6.4: Knowing thus, seeing thus, his mind [*citta*] is liberated from the *āsava* of sensual desire, from the *āsava* of existence, and from the *āsava* of blindness [*avijjā*];
an3.59:6.5: when liberated, there arises the knowledge: 'Liberated.'
an3.59:6.6: He understands: 'Birth is destroyed, the holy life has been lived, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this state of existence.'
an3.59:6.7: This is his third knowledge attained;
an3.59:6.8: blindness [*avijjā*] is destroyed, knowledge has arisen; darkness is destroyed, light has arisen — as it comes to one who dwells diligent, ardent, and resolute.
an3.59:7.1: One endowed with virtue and observances,
an3.59:7.3: Whose mind [*citta*] has come under mastery,
an3.59:8.2: and sees heaven and the lower realms;
an3.59:8.3: And who has reached the destruction of birth,
an3.59:8.4: the sage perfected through direct knowledge.
an3.59:9.2: a brahmin is one of the three knowledges;
an3.59:9.3: That one I call one of the three knowledges,
an3.59:9.4: not another who merely chatters and talks.
